location.office_time_required: up to one day per week from home (predominantly in-office)

level_signals: 8+ years of total relevant work experience required
Described as 'senior leader responsible for shaping and delivering large, complex, and high-impact strategic initiatives'
Ongoing engagement with senior stakeholders and executive sponsors
Base salary range $122,000–$261,000 depending on location, consistent with senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree or the equivalent combination of education and relevant experience AND
8+ years of total relevant work experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Preferred experience in a consultant and/or strategic planning role
Experience in Financial Services/Wealth Management industry a plus
Experience in managing successful relationships with senior leaders, cross-functional partners, and vendors
Strong project management and organizational skills; ability to successfully manage multiple projects simultaneously
Strong analytical skills and ability to think strategically and creatively
Proven ability to research, identify, interpret, and prioritize market needs
Experience in developing business cases and influencing change initiatives
Effective presentation and influencing skills, including strong written and verbal communication skills
Strong interpersonal skills - Works well in a collaborative, team-based environment
Demonstrated ability of successfully dealing with ambiguity and creating partnerships across business areas
Proven ability to navigate across organizations and develop partnerships along with gaining buy-in for strategic proposals
Demonstrated ability to build consensus at all levels
Ability to work effectively with a variety of functional areas: Advisory, Sales, Operations, Marketing, Client Experience, Service and Shared Services

explicitly_excluded: FINRA licenses are not required and will not be supported for this role.

scope.surface: Individual Investor (II) organization — Program and Change Management; manages programs for a business serving 1M+ investors and $200B+ in client assets
primary_metrics_inferred: Program delivery against timelines and budgets
KPIs and adoption metrics for strategic initiatives
Business case ROI and investment priorities
Client outcomes and growth of Individual Investor business
core_responsibilities: Lead end-to-end delivery of large, complex strategic programs within a financial services environment
Shape program strategy by developing business cases, defining multi-year roadmaps, and recommending investment priorities
Develop and manage integrated program plans, including scope, timelines, budgets, and resources
Establish and lead program governance, engaging senior stakeholders and executives
Proactively identify, assess, and mitigate risks, issues, and dependencies
Monitor program performance, including financials, KPIs, and adoption metrics
Strengthen organizational capabilities by promoting best practices, coaching teams, and enhancing program management methodologies

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed ($122K–$261K base depending on location)
High-visibility role with direct executive exposure
Large scope: $200B AUM and 1M+ investors creates meaningful impact
Role open until 08/30/2026 — long posting window suggests genuine need
Remote workers eligible for Maryland/Colorado/Washington pay band
red_flags: Hybrid policy allows only up to one day per week from home — effectively near-full-time in-office, primarily Owings Mills, MD
Most substantive qualifications are listed as 'Preferred' rather than 'Required', making the actual bar ambiguous
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed anywhere in the JD
Broad pay range ($122K–$261K) across geographies makes benchmarking difficult without knowing candidate's specific location
